title: AI compute stocks bounce as Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts
content: The deals sparked a rebound in AI compute stocks after investors questioned demand for new data center capacity.
summary: Hut 8's stock surged significantly following a $9.8 billion long-term lease for an AI data center. This substantial deal has reinvigorated investor confidence in the AI compute sector, which had previously faced skepticism regarding the demand for new data center capacity.

title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
content: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.
summary: Zcash has launched its new Zakura client, the first step in a plan to scale its private transaction capabilities from approximately one per second to 50,000 transactions per second. This initiative aims to achieve Visa-level privacy at payment-network scale.

title: Hut 8, IREN deals lift AI-focused Bitcoin mining stocks
content: Bitcoin miners rallied as investors cheered multi-billion-dollar AI infrastructure contracts, underscoring the sector’s accelerating shift toward data centers and cloud computing.
summary: Bitcoin mining stocks, including Hut 8 and IREN, rallied following the announcement of multi-billion-dollar AI infrastructure contracts. This development highlights an accelerating industry shift among Bitcoin miners towards data centers and cloud computing to support AI demands.

title: AI-assisted hacking is outpacing traditional code reviews, NEAR co-founder says
content: NEAR co-founder Polosukhin called formal verification a necessary shift for securing blockchain code from AI hacking.
summary: NEAR co-founder Polosukhin states that AI-assisted hacking is now outpacing traditional code reviews, posing a significant threat. He advocates for formal verification as a necessary shift to secure blockchain code against these advanced AI hacking methods.

title: Hut 8 fully commercializes 1 GW Texas AI campus with second $9.8B lease as IREN signs $2.8B in contracts; shares climb double digits
content: Hut 8 and IREN shares climb on fresh AI data center deals worth billions as the bitcoin-to-AI pivot continues to accelerate.
summary: Hut 8 has fully commercialized its 1 GW Texas AI campus with a second $9.8 billion lease, while IREN secured an additional $2.8 billion in contracts. These significant AI data center deals led to double-digit share increases for both companies, highlighting the accelerating industry pivot from bitcoin mining towards AI infrastructure.

title: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and what it means for users
content: The Van Rossem hard fork moved Cardano to version 11 on Saturday. And for the first time, Cardano's community voted in the upgrade, not the company that built it.
summary: Cardano successfully implemented its 'Van Rossum' hard fork on Saturday, upgrading the platform to version 11. This hard fork marks a significant milestone as it was the first time the Cardano community, rather than the developing company, voted on the upgrade.

title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slowed ether purchases as it bought back $86 million in stock
content: The company added just 7,430 ETH, worth about $14 million, last week as it nears its goal to corner 5% of Ethereum's supply.
summary: Tom Lee's Bitmine significantly slowed its Ethereum purchases last week, acquiring only 7,430 ETH worth $14 million. This reduction in crypto buying is linked to the company's decision to buy back $86 million in its own stock, even as it approaches its strategic goal of cornering 5% of Ethereum's total supply.

title: Saylor's Strategy raises cash reserves to $3.2 billion, leaving bitcoin holdings unchanged
content: For the second consecutive week, the firm kept bitcoin holdings steady, choosing to use the raised capital to boost cash for paying dividends on its preferred stock.
summary: Saylor's firm increased its cash reserves to $3.2 billion, utilizing raised capital to pay dividends on preferred stock. For the second consecutive week, the company maintained its bitcoin holdings without any changes.

title: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
content: The framework will address critical issues like official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility and system liability.
summary: Brazil's securities regulator has established a task force to develop a tokenization proposal within 60 days. This framework will tackle crucial issues such as official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility, and system liability for tokenized assets.

title: A bitcoin 'volmageddon' may be brewing, key indicator suggests
content: Your day-ahead look for July 20, 2026
summary: The article's title suggests a potential 'volmageddon' for Bitcoin, indicating that a period of extreme volatility may be imminent. A key indicator is reportedly signaling this brewing event, which could significantly impact the cryptocurrency market. This news serves as a forward-looking alert for Bitcoin investors and market participants.

title: Bank of Korea prepares for live CBDC transactions with 9 banks in September
content: Major banking heavyweights join a massive blockchain network test that will allow retail consumers to trade tokenized won freely across commercial lines.
summary: The Bank of Korea is preparing for live central bank digital currency (CBDC) transactions with 9 banks in September, allowing retail consumers to trade tokenized won freely. This move is part of a massive blockchain network test. Major banking heavyweights are participating in the test.

title: Cross-chain protocol Allbridge halts after $1.65 million flash loan exploit
content: The attacker used a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino to manipulate pool ratios, enabling them to withdraw assets at favorable rates before bridging funds.
summary: A cross-chain protocol called Allbridge was exploited for $1.65 million using a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino, allowing the attacker to manipulate pool ratios and withdraw assets at favorable rates. The attacker bridged funds after the manipulation. The exploit has caused Allbridge to halt operations.

title: U.S. regulatory developments and earnings, ECB rate decision: Crypto Week Ahead
content: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 20.
summary: The upcoming week will see significant events in the crypto space, including US regulatory developments and an ECB rate decision. These events may impact the cryptocurrency market. The week starting July 20 is expected to be notable for crypto enthusiasts.

title: Amazon Japan supplier AZ-Com Maruwa to adopt yen stablecoin JPYC for payments
content: The Japanese logistics firm plans to pay 2,300 partners, including truck drivers, using JPYC in Japan’s first large-scale corporate stablecoin rollout.
summary: Amazon Japan supplier AZ-Com Maruwa will use yen stablecoin JPYC for payments to 2,300 partners, including truck drivers, in Japan's first large-scale corporate stablecoin rollout. This move marks a significant adoption of cryptocurrency in the country's logistics industry. The use of JPYC is expected to streamline payments and increase efficiency.

title: Bitcoin's biggest advocate, Michael Saylor, says new plan to clean up the blockchain is 'a bad idea'
content: Michael Saylor argues that a new proposal, BIP-110, to temporarily block "spam" data from the Bitcoin blockchain would undermine the network's neutrality and create a dangerous precedent for censorship.
summary: Michael Saylor, a prominent Bitcoin advocate, has criticized a new proposal, BIP-110, which aims to temporarily block "spam" data from the Bitcoin blockchain. He argues it would undermine the network's neutrality and create a dangerous precedent for censorship.

title: AI is destroying the internet. Math is our only hope.
content: The rise of autonomous AI agents necessitates the utilization of zero-knowledge proofs, argues Brian Trunzo, chief growth officer at Succinct Labs.
summary: The article highlights the urgent need for zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) to address the challenges posed by the proliferation of autonomous AI agents, arguing that AI is negatively impacting the internet. Brian Trunzo of Succinct Labs suggests that advanced mathematical solutions like ZKPs are essential for AI's future integrity.

title: Tether's USDT hits 2-year countdown threatening its position on U.S. crypto platforms
content: The GENIUS Act has hit its first anniversary without U.S. regulators yet meeting deadlines to write regulations, though rules will be in full effect by July 2028.
summary: The GENIUS Act has reached its first anniversary, with U.S. regulators yet to meet deadlines for writing crucial regulations. Despite the current delays, the full rules are set to be in effect by July 2028, potentially threatening the position of stablecoins like Tether's USDT on U.S. crypto platforms.

title: Bitcoin’s quantum problem gets a recovery tool, but not for Satoshi’s 1.1 million coins
content: Project Eleven says it has funded a proof that lets a wallet's own key-derivation path stand in as ownership after quantum computers can forge its signatures. It runs in 243 milliseconds on a laptop.
summary: Project Eleven has developed a recovery tool for Bitcoin that allows a wallet's key-derivation path to serve as proof of ownership, specifically designed to counter signature forgery by future quantum computers. This solution runs quickly on a laptop but does not apply to Satoshi Nakamoto's 1.1 million Bitcoin.

title: Grayscale files S-1 for first US Worldcoin ETF
content: The filing expands Grayscale’s growing lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products outside of Bitcoin and Ether.
summary: Grayscale has filed an S-1 for a Worldcoin ETF, expanding its lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products beyond Bitcoin and Ether. This move indicates Grayscale's continued growth in the cryptocurrency market. The ETF is set to be the first of its kind in the US.

title: Celsius co-founders Leon, Goldstein to pay FTC over $6M
content: The settlements add to former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky’s $10 million FTC settlement in April.
summary: Celsius co-founders Leon and Goldstein have agreed to pay over $6M in settlements to the FTC, following former CEO Alex Mashinsky's $10 million settlement in April. This development is part of the ongoing regulatory actions against Celsius. The settlements highlight the ongoing scrutiny of cryptocurrency companies.

title: London Stock Exchange eyes overnight trading launch in 2027: FT
content: The move comes as traditional exchanges face growing competition from crypto markets and tokenized equity platforms that offer around-the-clock trading.
summary: The London Stock Exchange is considering launching overnight trading by 2027. This initiative is a direct response to increasing competition from crypto markets and tokenized equity platforms that offer continuous, around-the-clock trading.

title: Patrick Witt defers military training as CLARITY heads to Senate
content: The decision keeps the administration’s chief crypto policy negotiator in place during what could be a decisive few weeks for the CLARITY Act.
summary: Patrick Witt, the administration's chief crypto policy negotiator, has deferred military training to remain in place during a crucial period for the CLARITY Act. The CLARITY Act is heading to the Senate, and Witt's presence is significant for its negotiation. This decision allows Witt to continue his work on the Act.

title: Democrats added certain consumer protection rules to CLARITY: Coinbase exec
content: Coinbase vice chair Ryan VanGrack said that Democratic lawmakers had added provisions on customer protection to a digital asset market structure bill under consideration in the US Senate.
summary: Coinbase Vice Chair Ryan VanGrack announced that Democratic lawmakers have included customer protection provisions in a digital asset market structure bill currently under review in the US Senate. This indicates ongoing legislative efforts to establish regulations for the cryptocurrency industry.

title: Tether Gold recognized as Accepted Spot Commodity in Abu Dhabi financial center
content: The recognition allows regulated firms in Abu Dhabi Global Market to offer services involving Tether Gold as tokenized commodities gain broader adoption.
summary: Tether Gold has been officially recognized as an accepted spot commodity within the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM). This recognition permits regulated firms in ADGM to offer services involving Tether Gold, indicating a growing institutional acceptance for tokenized commodities.

title: SEC sues Mining Automatic and founder over alleged $22M crypto mining scheme
content: The SEC alleges Mining Automatic and its founder raised $22 million from investors by promising guaranteed crypto mining returns while spending only a fraction of the funds on mining operations.
summary: The SEC has sued Mining Automatic and its founder over an alleged $22M crypto mining scheme, claiming they raised funds from investors with promises of guaranteed returns, but spent only a fraction on actual mining operations. The scheme allegedly deceived investors, and the SEC is taking action. This lawsuit highlights concerns over crypto investment scams.

title: Nigerian president signs order on approach to crypto regulation, taxes
content: The West African country’s executive order established a virtual asset council and addressed the fragmentation of crypto regulation for oversight and enforcement.
summary: The Nigerian president has signed an executive order establishing a virtual asset council to address and streamline crypto regulation, taxation, oversight, and enforcement within the West African country. This move aims to unify the country's approach to virtual assets.

title: Bernstein raises Robinhood price target, cites tokenization and prediction markets
content: The brokerage’s blockchain strategy, including tokenized equities and its Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain, could reduce its reliance on traditional crypto trading, analysts said.
summary: Bernstein raised its price target for Robinhood, citing the brokerage's evolving blockchain strategy. This strategy includes tokenized equities and an Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain, which analysts believe could reduce its reliance on traditional crypto trading while still leveraging the blockchain space.

title: IREN jumps 16% after raising AI cloud revenue target above $4B
content: The Bitcoin miner raised its year-end AI cloud revenue target to more than $4 billion after signing $2.8 billion in new contracts with AI developers.
summary: IREN, a Bitcoin miner, experienced a 16% stock surge after raising its year-end AI cloud revenue target to over $4 billion. This significant increase follows the signing of $2.8 billion in new contracts with AI developers, signaling a major expansion into the AI infrastructure market.

title: Strategy raises $263.5M through MSTR sales, holds 843,775 Bitcoin
content: Strategy boosted its cash reserve to $3.225 billion after selling MSTR shares, while investors weighed the value of its STRC preferred stock.
summary: Strategy has raised $263.5 million by selling MSTR shares and now holds a cash reserve of $3.225 billion, while also holding 843,775 Bitcoin. This move may impact the value of its STRC preferred stock. The sale has bolstered Strategy's cash position.

title: Zilliqa asks exchanges to pause ZIL transfers after suspected cold wallet theft
content: Zilliqa said exchanges paused ZIL deposits and withdrawals after an exchange partner’s cold wallet was compromised, with the amount stolen still undisclosed.
summary: Zilliqa has asked exchanges to pause ZIL transfers due to a suspected cold wallet theft, with the amount stolen not yet disclosed. The pause on ZIL deposits and withdrawals was made after an exchange partner's cold wallet was compromised. The incident is currently being investigated.

title: Crypto institutions look beyond audits as trust signals falter: Hacken
content: Institutional due diligence is shifting toward continuous monitoring, signer controls and incident readiness after operational failures accounted for most crypto losses.
summary: Crypto institutions are changing their approach to security and trust, moving beyond audits to continuous monitoring and incident readiness, as operational failures have been the main cause of losses in the industry. This shift is in response to the limitations of traditional audits in preventing security breaches. The new approach aims to provide more robust protection for crypto assets.

title: Russia’s parliament to hold final readings on crypto bill Tuesday
content: Russia’s State Duma is set to consider the crypto regulation bill in second and third readings, with rules for investors and cross-border payments included.
summary: Russia's State Duma will hold final readings on a crypto regulation bill, which includes rules for investors and cross-border payments. The bill aims to establish a regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies in Russia. This is a significant step towards legitimizing crypto in the country.

title: Grayscale plans regular cash payouts from ETH, SOL staking rewards
content: Grayscale plans to establish regular cash distributions from staking rewards generated by its Ether and Solana exchange-traded products.
summary: Grayscale plans to start making regular cash payouts from the staking rewards of its Ether and Solana exchange-traded products. This move indicates Grayscale's efforts to provide returns to its investors. The payouts will come from the staking rewards generated by these products.

title: South Korea eyes September launch for second phase of CBDC pilot: Report
content: The Bank of Korea’s CBDC pilot will add two regional banks, new payment features and tests of government subsidy payments using tokenized bank deposits
summary: The Bank of Korea is planning to launch the second phase of its central bank digital currency (CBDC) pilot in September, which will include two regional banks and new payment features. The pilot will also test government subsidy payments using tokenized bank deposits. This marks a significant step in the development of CBDCs in South Korea.

title: Cardano activates van Rossem hard fork
content: The upgrade reduces smart contract execution costs while laying the groundwork for Ouroboros Leios, a major scalability upgrade expected later this year.
summary: Cardano has activated the Van Rossem hard fork, which reduces smart contract execution costs and sets the stage for Ouroboros Leios, a major scalability upgrade expected later this year. This upgrade is significant for the Cardano network and its users. The move aims to improve the overall efficiency of the network.

title: Allbridge pauses cross-chain bridge after $1.65M exploit
content: The attacker allegedly used a flash loan and rapid swaps to manipulate the bridge’s stablecoin exchange rate.
summary: Allbridge Core has paused its cross-chain bridge following a $1.65 million exploit. The attacker reportedly used a flash loan and rapid swaps to manipulate the bridge's stablecoin exchange rate.

title: SEC charges Florida man and company in alleged $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme
content: The defendants allegedly raised $22 million from more than 380 investors through a fraudulent crypto asset mining scheme.
summary: The SEC has charged a Florida man and his company with allegedly running a $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme, deceiving over 380 investors. The scheme involved false promises of crypto asset mining returns. The defendants are accused of raising funds through fraudulent means.

title: Trump agrees to ethics provision as crypto bill inches closer to Senate vote
content: President Trump has agreed to ethics language — potentially moving the ball forward to getting crypto legislation passed into law.
summary: President Trump has agreed to ethics language in a crypto bill, which may help move the legislation forward to a Senate vote. The bill's progress could potentially lead to the passage of cryptocurrency legislation into law. This development is a significant step for the crypto community.

title: Grayscale could take Worldcoin to Wall Street after ETF filing with the SEC
content: The first exchange-traded fund tied to Worldcoin could soon be headed to U.S. markets, as Grayscale files for a Worldcoin ETF.
summary: Grayscale has filed for a Worldcoin ETF with the SEC, which could lead to the first exchange-traded fund tied to Worldcoin being listed on US markets. This move could increase accessibility to Worldcoin for traditional investors. The ETF would track the performance of Worldcoin.

title: Bitmine expands Ethereum treasury to 5.78 million ETH, repurchases 5.5 million shares
content: Bitmine expanded its ether treasury to 5.78 million ETH and repurchased 5.5 million shares under its $4 billion buyback program.
summary: Bitmine has expanded its Ethereum treasury to 5.78 million ETH and repurchased 5.5 million shares as part of its $4 billion buyback program. This move signifies a significant investment in Ethereum. The buyback program aims to reduce the company's outstanding shares.

title: Bernstein lifts Robinhood target to $160, sees prediction markets revenue overtaking crypto
content: Bernstein raised its Robinhood price target to $160, citing prediction markets, Rothera, and Robinhood Chain as key revenue drivers.
summary: Bernstein has increased its target price for Robinhood to $160, citing revenue growth from prediction markets, Rothera, and Robinhood Chain. Prediction markets are expected to become a significant revenue source, potentially overtaking crypto. This move indicates a shift in focus towards new revenue streams.

title: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin ‘vibe-codes’ anonymous billboard demo with onchain moderation on Aztec
content: Vitalik Buterin "vibe-coded" an anonymous billboard demo on Aztec with onchain moderation, a censor role, and a local LLM flagging daemon.
summary: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has demonstrated an anonymous billboard on Aztec, utilizing onchain moderation and other features. The demo included a censor role and a local LLM flagging daemon. This showcases potential applications of blockchain and AI technologies.

title: Allbridge Core pauses protocol after $1.65 million flash loan exploit: onchain analysts
content: Security firms PeckShield and CertiK flagged that the attacker has bridged the stolen funds from Solana to Ethereum.
summary: The Allbridge Core protocol has been paused after a $1.65 million flash loan exploit, with the attacker bridging the stolen funds from Solana to Ethereum. Security firms PeckShield and CertiK have flagged the incident. The exploit highlights the ongoing security risks in the cryptocurrency space.

title: Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal
Bitcoin miner Hut 8 has said it signed a second 15-year lease ​worth $9.8 billion for its AI data center. 
This post Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Hut 8, a Bitcoin miner, has signed a 15-year lease worth $9.8 billion for its AI data center, causing its shares to jump. The deal is significant for the company and its operations. This news indicates a growing intersection between cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence.

title: Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate
Coinbase's Ryan VanGrack said the Clarity Act is gaining strong Senate momentum and argued it would bring long-awaited federal rules, stronger consumer protections, and regulatory clarity to the crypto industry.
This post Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: A Coinbase executive reported that the Clarity Act is gaining momentum in the Senate, which could bring federal rules and regulatory clarity to the crypto industry. The act is expected to provide stronger consumer protections. This development is seen as a positive step for the crypto community.

title: Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation
Russia is pushing ahead with regulating the cryptocurrency market, with the State Duma considering a bill on combating fraud in the industry, according to reports.  The bill will look at “combating the illegal use of cryptocurrencies within our country,” Anatoly Aksakov, chairman of the State Duma Committee on Financial Markets, reportedly said. Lawmakers will vote […]
This post Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Russia is considering a bill to combat crypto fraud, aiming to regulate the cryptocurrency market and prevent illegal use of cryptocurrencies within the country. The bill is being reviewed by the State Duma and will be put to a vote. This move is part of Russia's efforts to rein in crypto fraud and establish clearer guidelines for the industry.

title: Strategy (MSTR) Sells $263.5 Million in MSTR Shares, Skips Bitcoin Purchase as USD Reserve Tops $3.2 Billion
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strategy (MSTR) Sells $263.5 Million in MSTR Shares, Skips Bitcoin Purchase as USD Reserve Tops $3.2 Billion
Strategy sold $263.5 million in MSTR shares last week without buying bitcoin, raising its U.S. dollar reserve to a record $3.225 billion while maintaining its 843,775 BTC treasury and continuing its recent shift toward cash accumulation.
This post Strategy (MSTR) Sells $263.5 Million in MSTR Shares, Skips Bitcoin Purchase as USD Reserve Tops $3.2 Billion first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strategy (MSTR) sold $263.5 million in MSTR shares and increased its USD reserve to $3.225 billion, while maintaining its bitcoin treasury at 843,775 BTC. The company chose not to buy additional bitcoin. This move represents a shift towards cash accumulation.

title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s…
summary: A study finds that AI is more likely to form biases when hiring than humans, which can affect the job screening process. This discovery highlights a significant issue in the use of AI in hiring. The finding has implications for the fairness of AI-powered hiring tools.

title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
content: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also develop their own biases from…
summary: Researchers have found that AI, specifically large language models (LLMs), can develop biases when screening job applicants, potentially leading to unfair hiring practices. This is because LLMs can pick up human biases from their training data and also create their own biases. As a result, AI may be more likely to form biases than humans when hiring.

title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other…
summary: OpenAI has developed GPT-Red, a large language model designed to test and improve the safety of its other models by acting as a sparring partner. This innovation aims to enhance the security and reliability of OpenAI's technologies. GPT-Red is used to simulate hacking attempts and identify potential vulnerabilities.

title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
content: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its most robust release yet. GPT-Red automates…
summary: OpenAI has developed GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker, to test and improve the security of its other models against cyberattacks, resulting in the robust GPT-5.6 release. GPT-Red acts as a sparring partner to boost the defenses of OpenAI's models. This breakthrough aims to enhance the safety of OpenAI's models.

title: The Download: a useful quantum machine and a record-breaking subsea tunnel
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data…
summary: PsiQuantum is developing a massive quantum computer using light, which could potentially change the world, and will be housed in a room resembling a data center. This innovation is part of the daily technology newsletter, The Download. The quantum machine has the potential to significantly impact various fields, including possibly AI and crypto.

title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.
summary: A $1.5B copyright settlement between Anthropic and a party involved in a case about using copyrighted works to train AI models has been approved. This settles one case, but the broader issue remains unresolved. The settlement's impact on the AI community is significant.

title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
content: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.
summary: Google's parent company Alphabet is developing a new AI chip to improve the efficiency of its Gemini models. This chip aims to enhance performance while reducing resource usage. The development is part of Google's ongoing efforts to advance its AI capabilities.

title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
content: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.
summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is being improved for easier use, it's a fundamental protocol for AI interoperability, enabling secure access to external data sources and services. This upgrade facilitates smoother interactions between AI models and external tools. MCP acts as a standard interface, reducing the need for custom engineering

title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
content: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.
summary: OpenAI is concerned about open-weight models, particularly those made in China, which poses a challenge in turning AI into a business. The US is considering banning these models, highlighting the complexities of AI development and regulation. This issue reveals the tension between open-source AI models and commercialization.

title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
content: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.
summary: A lawsuit from Apple may impact OpenAI's plans to enter the hardware market and go public, according to a recent episode of Equity. The lawsuit's outcome could have significant implications for OpenAI's future. The discussion on Equity explores the potential effects on OpenAI's hardware ambitions.

title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
content: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."
summary: Director Christopher Nolan has publicly voiced his concern regarding artificial intelligence, labeling it an 'obvious Trojan horse'. He suggested that the inherent risks or hidden intentions of AI are apparent, stating that 'everybody knows the Greeks are inside'.

title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
content: Current AI, a nonprofit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat, and more.
summary: Current AI, a non-profit organization, is actively working to create a 'World Wide Web of AI' that is free and accessible to everyone, ensuring no culture is left behind. They have reported significant progress in areas such as AI chat and device compatibility.

title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
content: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."
summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI has released a new version of its Kimi model, sparking concerns about the potential implications of advanced AI. The concept of 'full AI communism' has been raised, implying a significant shift in the way AI systems are developed and utilized. This development has notable implications for the AI community.

title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
content: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open-weight AI models for coding.
summary: Databricks has transformed into an AI company and published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding, achieving a valuation of $188B. This development solidifies its position in the AI industry. The company's focus on AI has led to significant growth and recognition.

title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
content: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.
summary: India's smartphone market is experiencing a slowdown due to the AI boom, which is changing consumer electronics in terms of pricing, demand, and corporate strategy. The AI-driven shift is impacting the market, with significant effects on consumer behavior and company approaches. This change highlights the growing influence of AI on various industries.

title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
content: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]
summary: Apple has filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct and claiming over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI, which could disrupt OpenAI's potential IPO plans. The lawsuit's timing is particularly noteworthy given OpenAI's reported interest in going public. The outcome of the lawsuit could have significant implications for OpenAI's future

title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
content:
Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this list represents "only a small portion of Plaintiffs' works that Udio infringed," as reported earlier by Music Business Worldwide.
In 2024, Sony, along with Universal Music Group and Warner Records, filed a lawsuit against Udio and the AI music-maker Suno. After gaining access to Udio's training data through discovery, Sony cla …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed a lawsuit against Udio, an AI music generator, for copyright infringement of over 30,000 songs, including works by Elvis Presley, Beyoncé, and Harry Styles. The lawsuit claims this is only a small portion of the infringed works. Sony had previously filed a lawsuit against Udio and another AI music-maker in 2024.

title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
content:
Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe	

Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models.
Adobe describes the new "AI Playground" tooling suite as an experiment and says there's a button that allows users to opt out and continue using the app as before. Free access to the suite with no sign-on requirement is being tested with a small percentage of Indigo users over the next few weeks. "Our goal is to learn how people …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Adobe's Indigo camera app is being updated with generative AI tools, allowing users to opt out and continue using the app as before. The update is currently being tested with a small percentage of users. The goal is to learn how people use the new AI features.

title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
content:
China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. 
The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which unveiled Kimi K3 on Friday. Moonshot claims its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: China's AI companies, such as Moonshot and Alibaba, are releasing models that can compete with top US models at a lower cost, challenging America's lead in AI. This development has significant implications for national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. China's rapid progress in AI may erode the US's dominance in the field.

title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
content:
TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool and recently made it available to all adult users.
Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with a company called Jumio. You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: TikTok is testing a tool that detects AI-generated likenesses, allowing creators to report them, with the goal of combating AI-generated content. The tool is currently being tested with some US creators and requires identity verification. This move follows YouTube's similar initiative to tackle AI-generated content.

title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
content:
Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI?
On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David go through the lawsuit, and look at Apple's history of splashy litigation to determine whether Apple is worried about a possible competitor or simply looking to capitalize on a weak moment for OpenAI. All this is happening as Apple ships the public betas of its new software, headlined by the new Siri AI, and we have though …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, with many experts thinking the allegations are standard practices, and the lawsuit's true intentions are unclear, possibly due to Apple seeing OpenAI as a competitor or capitalizing on a weak moment. The lawsuit is happening as Apple ships its new software featuring a new Siri AI. The Verge's Nilay and David analyzed the lawsuit to understand Apple's motivations.

title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
content:
New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy" to check for outdated legislation.
Some of the antiquated laws mentioned by Hochul in the interview include a $25 fee required to take a dog hunting, or a stipulation that pregnant people need a permit to work after midnight. Hochul added that it "probably would have taken five years at the staff level" to review all of the laws in the stat …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: New York Governor Kathy Hochul is utilizing AI to review and analyze the state's rules and regulations to identify outdated legislation, including a $25 fee for taking a dog hunting and a permit requirement for pregnant people working after midnight. This effort aims to streamline and modernize the state's laws. The use of AI is expected to significantly reduce the time required to review the laws.

title: Beyond grep: The case for a context-rich AI coding harness
content: Augment Code's Vinay Perneti talks models, harnesses, and context.
summary: Vinay Perneti from Augment Code discusses the potential of AI in coding, suggesting a context-rich AI coding harness that goes beyond traditional tools like grep. This approach aims to improve coding efficiency and accuracy. The conversation involves models and harnesses in the context of AI-assisted coding.

title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
content: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.
summary: The government is piloting a program that leverages AI for insurance-coverage decisions, particularly concerning prior authorization. This initiative raises questions about whether AI will improve the efficiency of these processes or introduce new challenges.

title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
content: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.
summary: The EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android, which Google claims could compromise user privacy and security. This move is part of the EU's efforts to regulate big tech companies. The changes aim to promote competition and transparency in the tech industry.

title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.

This comes after xAI claimed it could no longer deny that Grok was being used for this purpose. The lawsuit highlights the challenges faced by AI companies in regulating user-generated content.

title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
content: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.

The strike highlights concerns about the impact of automation on employment. The introduction of Atlas robots is expected to significantly change the factory workforce.

title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."

This response indicates a strong stance in favor of AI integration in Linux. The decision may have significant implications for the future of Linux development.

summary: A lawsuit claims that Meta used AI to make layoff decisions, potentially targeting workers with disabilities and medical problems. Meta denies the allegations, stating that humans made the termination decisions. The lawsuit raises concerns about the use of AI in employment decisions.

This moratorium may hinder the growth and development of AI technologies that rely on data centers. The ban's impact on the AI industry is likely to be closely watched by experts and stakeholders.

title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
content: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.
summary: Defenders are using a technique called 'context bombing' or 'prompt injection' to trick hacking agents into shutting down before they can cause harm. This method involves providing misleading context to the hacking agents. The technique is showing promise in preventing cyber attacks.

title: Simulating everything, sort of: The promise and limits of world models
content: Experts explain how they work, what they can do, and what's still unsettled.
summary: Experts discuss the capabilities and limitations of world models, which are simulating complex systems. These models can mimic various aspects of reality, but their potential and current state are still being explored. World models have significant implications for fields like artificial intelligence.

title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out

title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
content:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The author, usually critical of generative AI music, expresses surprise and enjoyment for 1010Benja's song "Semiramis' Dream," which was created using AI, specifically Suno. 1010Benja has been open about using generative AI in his latest EP, and this particular track challenges the author's general negative perception of AI-produced music.

title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
content:
Dave Eggers attends the "The Turning Point: To Be Destroyed" premiere. | Image: John Lamparski/Getty Images for Tribeca Festival	

Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the Financial Times, Eggers told the staff:
"The effect of ChatGPT on educators' lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you've made every teache …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Author Dave Eggers, invited by Sam Altman, criticized ChatGPT during a talk to OpenAI staff, stating its impact on educators is "catastrophic." He reportedly argued that the AI tool is "silencing an entire generation," challenging the company despite being expected to offer advice on productivity.

title: Crypto market slips even as equities advance. Pump surges on social-media chatter
content: With a Fear and Greed index pointing solidly at "fear," crypto prices drifted lower even as stock index futures rose. PUMP's 20% surge was the only real headline in an otherwise quiet session.
summary: Crypto prices fell despite rising stock index futures, with the Fear and Greed index indicating fear, while PUMP surged 20% due to social media chatter. The crypto market's decline occurred in an otherwise quiet session. The surge in PUMP was the notable event in the market.

summary: Moonshot is moving towards a Hong Kong listing with a valuation of over $30 billion, following the release of Kimi K3 and Alibaba's Qwen. This comes after Kimi K3's release shook the chip stocks. The listings and releases have also impacted bitcoin.

summary: Capital B, Europe's second-biggest Bitcoin treasury company, has approved a 10-for-1 reverse stock split to attract more institutional investors. The move is expected to broaden the company's investor base. The reverse stock split is set to take place in September.

This move seeks to leverage the benefits of stablecoins in streamlining payment processes. The firm's strategy may help alleviate labor issues by providing more appealing payment terms.

content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet subsidiary Bitcoin Japan is starting a crypto treasury.

title: Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September
Capital B will execute a 10-for-1 reverse stock split in September as the Paris-listed bitcoin treasury company looks to attract more institutional investors while continuing to build its 3,139 BTC reserve.
This post Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Onramp Report Makes the Case for Spot Bitcoin Over Paper Claims as Price Sits at Half Its Peak
Onramp argues that spot bitcoin offers stronger long-term value than paper bitcoin exposure, highlighting bitcoin's fixed supply as its price trades roughly 50% below its all-time high.
This post New Onramp Report Makes the Case for Spot Bitcoin Over Paper Claims as Price Sits at Half Its Peak first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

The report highlights the benefits of investing in spot bitcoin. This comes as bitcoin's price continues to be below its peak.

title: Strive (ASST) Adds 21 Bitcoin as Treasury Reaches 19,921 BTC and Cash Climbs to $157 Million
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strive (ASST) Adds 21 Bitcoin as Treasury Reaches 19,921 BTC and Cash Climbs to $157 Million
Strive (ASST) bought 21 bitcoin for about $1.3 million last week, increasing its treasury to 19,921 BTC while cash reserves rose to $157.4 million, according to a Monday SEC filing.

Bitcoin Magazine

title: Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1%
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1%
Ocean Mining’s VP of Development and Engineering Jason Hughes argues BIP-110 lacks measurable consensus, with node signaling at 7-15% and hashrate support under 1% ahead of the critical block 961632 window.
This post Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1% first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Jason Hughes.
summary: Jason Hughes, Ocean Mining's VP, believes BIP-110 will fail due to lack of consensus, with node signaling at 7-15% and hashrate support under 1%. The proposal is nearing a critical block window. Hughes argues that the current support is not enough for the proposal to pass.

summary: Adobe's camera app has introduced a new feature using AI to critique photos and remove backgrounds. This feature, part of Project Indigo, allows for the removal of various backgrounds from photos taken with the app. The AI-powered tool enhances photo editing capabilities.

title: YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos
content: YouTube has updated its monetization policies to more clearly define the kinds of AI-generated and low-quality videos that can’t earn ad revenue.
summary: YouTube has updated its monetization policies to clarify what types of AI-generated and low-quality videos are ineligible for ad revenue. This change aims to improve video quality on the platform. The policy update affects creators who produce AI-generated content.

title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
content: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.
summary: Jensen Huang's visit to Japan has resulted in multiple deals across the country's tech ecosystem. The details of the deals are not specified, but they span the entire ecosystem. This could have significant implications for the tech industry.

title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
content: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?
summary: A potential hack in Zoom meetings raises concerns about privacy and data usage, as meetings are being transcribed and summarized, leading to questions about who has access to the information. The article touches on the idea that with so much data being recorded, it's unclear who is actually reading and utilizing it. This hack highlights the need for transparency in data collection and usage.

title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
content: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.
summary: Agility Robotics is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California, near Tesla's headquarters. This move signifies Agility's expansion and commitment to robotics development. The center will likely facilitate training and innovation for the company's robotic products.

title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
content: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.
summary: A competition in California, in collaboration with XPRIZE, is testing the use of drones to stop wildfires early, as the US faces nearly year-round wildfires. The goal is to utilize drones for firefighting purposes. This initiative aims to find innovative solutions to combat wildfires.

title: Google-backed satellites for wildfire detection launch as smoke chokes US, Canada
content: The FireSat program can spot wildfires that other satellites miss.
summary: Google-backed satellites have launched as part of the FireSat program to detect wildfires, which can spot fires that other satellites miss. The launch comes as smoke from wildfires affects the US and Canada. This technology uses artificial intelligence to quickly identify fires.

title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
content: Companies coming to market are raising money at fastest pace this century.
summary: Energy companies are going public at a rapid pace, raising funds at the fastest rate this century, as investors seek to capitalize on the AI boom. This surge in energy IPOs indicates a growing interest in the sector, driven by the potential of AI to transform the industry. The trend is expected to continue, with more energy companies planning to go public in the near future.

title: OpenAI's first branded hardware is... a light-up keyboard?
content: The Codex Micro is designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance.
summary: OpenAI has released its first branded hardware, a light-up keyboard called the Codex Micro, designed to monitor multiple agentic threads at a glance. This unusual product may indicate the company's interest in exploring new areas beyond software. The Codex Micro's purpose and functionality are not yet fully understood.

title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
content: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.
summary: The US military has used explosive drone boats in combat for the first time, targeting an Iranian naval port amidst escalating tensions. This marks a new development in naval warfare technology. The use of drone boats could have significant implications for future conflicts.

title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
content: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.
summary: Google is revamping its image search for its 25th anniversary, using more AI to create personalized galleries based on users' unique interests. The new feature will provide an always-updated gallery of images. This update aims to improve user experience with more relevant results.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides a daily roundup of the latest news affecting the cryptocurrency market, covering topics such as Bitcoin price movements, blockchain developments, DeFi, Web3, and regulatory updates. It serves as a quick guide to current trends and events in the crypto space.

title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
content: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.
summary: Vertu is offering a luxury foldable phone equipped with an AI agent to executives for $6,880. The article reviews the device's daily performance, specifically examining its AI workflows, battery life, and security features.

title: The GENIUS Act turns 1: State of Crypto
content: The stablecoin-focused GENIUS Act became a law a year ago.
summary: The GENIUS Act, a legislative initiative primarily focused on stablecoins, recently marked its first anniversary since becoming law. This milestone signifies one year of the act's implementation in the regulatory landscape concerning cryptocurrency.

title: Bitcoin price hits $65K wall as stocks battle ‘record’ institutional tech sell-off
content: Bitcoin faced troublesome resistance at $65,000 while traders still saw a potential BTC price breakout into a “bullish market structure.”
summary: Bitcoin encountered significant resistance at the $65,000 mark. Despite this, traders remain hopeful for a potential price breakout that could lead to a bullish market structure for BTC.

title: Washington judge blocks Kalshi’s sports prediction markets over state gambling laws
content: A Washington state court granted the state's motion for a preliminary injunction, saying Kalshi operates illegal gambling activities.
summary: A Washington state court has blocked Kalshi's sports prediction markets, deeming them illegal gambling activities under state law. The decision was made after the state filed a motion for a preliminary injunction. Kalshi's operations will be halted until further notice.

title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
content: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in many industries, with real money, livelihoods, and even actual lives at stake. Farmers use…
summary: Weather forecasts significantly impact various industries such as aviation, energy, and agriculture, influencing strategic decisions with substantial financial and human implications. The accuracy of these forecasts is crucial, and any disruption could have far-reaching consequences. However, the article highlights the rising risk of weather data sabotage.

title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
content: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.
summary: X has released a rebuilt version of its Android app after a year-long effort, and it is now available globally. The app has been revamped, but details about the changes are not provided. This release aims to improve user experience.

title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
content:
AUUUUUGH | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble, and, in my opinion, terribly overpriced - is fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100? Does it suddenly threaten the stability of index funds based on the Nasdaq-100? Can a $1.77 trillion IPO crater the retirement funds of regular people who would ordinarily have no interest in investing in Elon Musk's meme stock?
The answer has less to do with SpaceX and more to do with index funds - how they work, their history, and why they've …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The inclusion of SpaceX in the Nasdaq-100 index fund has raised concerns about the stability of such funds, particularly for ordinary investors. However, the impact may be less about SpaceX itself and more about how index funds work. The article explores the mechanics and history of index funds to address these concerns.

title: San Francisco orders Apple, Google to remove nudify apps from app stores
content: Official estimates Google and Apple likely made millions in nudify app fees.
summary: San Francisco has ordered Apple and Google to remove nudify apps from their app stores, with estimates suggesting the tech giants made millions in fees from these apps. The move is aimed at addressing concerns over the apps' content. The decision may impact the companies' revenue streams.

title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.
content: Perimenopause has entered the chat. Perimenopause—and its better-known relative, menopause—used to be considered taboo. Not anymore, thanks at least in part to TV doctors and social media influencers. Perhaps it’s my age, but these days, both my algorithm and my conversations with friends increasingly swing toward perimenopause. Menopause is defined as the life stage that…
summary: The article discusses the increasing awareness and discussion of perimenopause, a life stage that was previously considered taboo, now being openly talked about due to TV doctors and social media influencers. Perimenopause is the stage leading up to menopause, and its definition and implications are being explored. The article seems to be skeptical of the hype surrounding perimenopause.

title: Why heat pumps are still so hot in the US
content: It feels as if it should be illegal to even think about heating appliances during the height of summer—seriously, these heat waves in New York have been brutal—but we need to talk about heat pumps. The appliances use electricity for heating, they’re incredibly efficient, and they’re on the rise. (For what it’s worth, many heat…
summary: The article discusses the increasing popularity of heat pumps in the US, highlighting their efficiency and rise in usage. Heat pumps use electricity for heating and are becoming more prominent. The topic is unrelated to AI or crypto, focusing on home appliances.

title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 136, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been recording the next season of Version History (this season's finale is out on Sunday!), reading about data center heists and Backyard Baseball and the creator of Calvin and Hobbes, canceling my October plans to see Digger 30 or 40 times, taking on the new Knockout Tour routes in Mario Kart World, learning more than I ever intended about Staten Island thanks to Revisionist His …

summary: The article is an introduction to "Installer No. 136," a column from The Verge providing a guide to the latest tech and culture. The author shares personal updates, including recording a podcast, reading about diverse topics like data center heists, and playing games, inviting readers to explore the full story.
